title: ulmus glabra f. monstrosa
intro: Ulmus glabra f. monstrosa, also known as the Wych Elm, is a deciduous tree species that is a variant of the Ulmus glabra, characterized by its broad, vase-shaped canopy and unique, irregular growth habit. The Wych Elm has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The medicinal properties of Ulmus glabra f. monstrosa have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, while also providing antioxidant and cardiovascular benefits. In herbal preparations, the bark, leaves, and inner sap of the Wych Elm are often used to create teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its health benefits.
